metadata.title: Why the server hands back an encrypted context you're only going to echo
metadata.topic: Streaming context compaction — readable summary vs. sealed token
metadata.purpose: The readable text is display/debug only; the encrypted blob is a server-verifiable token that reconstructs full compressed context — turn boundaries, roles, metadata — that plain prose throws away.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: raster (message stack → single block), c2v (one event → two stored values, distinct fates)
metadata.exclusions: compaction thresholds, the encryption algorithm, manual-compaction APIs; the Python fixture duplicate
metadata.visual_object: A tall message stack collapsing into one block that emits two outputs — an open speech bubble and a sealed box; only the box feeds the next call.
metadata.hook: When history overflows, compaction returns TWO things: a human-readable summary and an opaque encrypted blob — and only one of them keeps the conversation's memory.

beats.narration_text: A `compaction` block carries `content: "Earlier conversation summarized."` next to `encrypted_content: "EpwBCioIDxgC..."`; thread the readable text back and context breaks; thread the blob back and it holds.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If the readable summary is what the model "remembered," why does passing it (instead of the encrypted blob) into the next call lose context?
When history overflows, compaction returns TWO things: a human-readable summary and an opaque encrypted blob — and only one of them keeps the conversation's memory.
The readable text is display/debug only; the encrypted blob is a server-verifiable token that reconstructs full compressed context — turn boundaries, roles, metadata — that plain prose throws away.
beats.visual: A tall message stack collapsing into one block that emits two outputs — an open speech bubble and a sealed box; only the box feeds the next call.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 20-turn chat → compaction fires → yields summary text + `encrypted_content: "Epw..."`; next call passes only the blob; server rebuilds full context from the token, not a 20-message replay.
The readable text is display/debug only; the encrypted blob is a server-verifiable token that reconstructs full compressed context — turn boundaries, roles, metadata — that plain prose throws away.
